016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.mock;
018
019import java.io.*;
020import java.nio.charset.*;
021import java.util.*;
022import java.util.concurrent.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.rest.client.*;
025
026/**
027 * A programmable in-process {@link HttpTransport} for unit testing.
028 *
029 * <p>
030 * Register one or more route handlers, or a catch-all handler, and the transport will dispatch
031 * each {@link TransportRequest} to the appropriate handler without any real network activity.
032 *
033 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
034 * <p class='bjava'>
035 *    <jv>transport</jv> = MockHttpTransport.<jsm>builder</jsm>()
036 *       .on(<js>"GET"</js>, <js>"/hello"</js>, req -&gt; TransportResponse.<jsm>builder</jsm>()
037 *          .statusCode(200)
038 *          .reasonPhrase(<js>"OK"</js>)
039 *          .header(<js>"Content-Type"</js>, <js>"text/plain"</js>)
040 *          .body(new ByteArrayInputStream(<js>"Hello, World!"</js>.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8)))
041 *          .build())
042 *       .build();
043 *
044 *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>builder</jsm>()
045 *       .transport(<jv>transport</jv>)
046 *       .build();
047 * </p>
048 *
049 * <p>
050 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
051 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
052 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
053 * (and possibly earlier).
054 *
055 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
056 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
057 * </ul>
058 *
059 * @since 9.2.1
060 */
061@SuppressWarnings({
062   "resource" // execute()/of()/build() return Closeable types; caller must close TransportResponse / MockHttpTransport
063})
064public final class MockHttpTransport implements HttpTransport {
065
066   /**
067    * A handler that maps an incoming {@link TransportRequest} to a {@link TransportResponse}.
068    *
069    * @since 9.2.1
070    */
071   @FunctionalInterface
072   public interface RequestHandler {
073      /**
074       * Handles the request.
075       *
076       * @param request The incoming request. Never {@code null}.
077       * @return The response to return. Never {@code null}.
078       * @throws TransportException If the handler wants to simulate a network error.
079       */
080      TransportResponse handle(TransportRequest request) throws TransportException;
081   }
082
083   private final List<Route> routes;
084   private final RequestHandler fallback;
085   private final List<TransportRequest> recordedRequests;
086
087   private MockHttpTransport(Builder builder) {
088      this.routes = List.copyOf(builder.routes);
089      this.fallback = builder.fallback;
090      this.recordedRequests = builder.recordRequests ? new CopyOnWriteArrayList<>() : null;
091   }
092
093   /** Returns a new {@link Builder}. */
094   public static Builder builder() {
095      return new Builder();
096   }
097
098   /**
099    * Creates a simple mock transport that always returns the given status code and body string.
100    *
101    * @param statusCode The HTTP status code to return.
102    * @param body The body string to return (UTF-8 encoded). May be <jk>null</jk>.
103    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
104    */
105   public static MockHttpTransport of(int statusCode, String body) {
106      return builder()
107         .fallback((RequestHandler) req -> TransportResponse.builder()
108            .statusCode(statusCode)
109            .body(body != null ? new ByteArrayInputStream(body.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8)) : null)
110            .build())
111         .build();
112   }
113
114   @Override /* HttpTransport */
115   public TransportResponse execute(TransportRequest request) throws TransportException {
116      if (recordedRequests != null)
117         recordedRequests.add(request);
118
119      var path = request.getUri().getPath();
120      var method = request.getMethod();
121
122      for (var route : routes) {
123         if (route.matches(method, path))
124            return route.handler().handle(request);
125      }
126
127      if (fallback != null)
128         return fallback.handle(request);
129
130      return TransportResponse.builder()
131         .statusCode(404)
132         .reasonPhrase("Not Found")
133         .body(new ByteArrayInputStream(("No mock route matched: " + method + " " + path).getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8)))
134         .build();
135   }
136
137   /**
138    * Returns an unmodifiable view of all requests received since creation (only available when recording is enabled).
139    *
140    * @return The recorded requests. Empty if recording is disabled.
141    */
142   public List<TransportRequest> getRecordedRequests() {
143      return recordedRequests != null ? Collections.unmodifiableList(recordedRequests) : List.of();
144   }
145
146   /**
147    * Clears the list of recorded requests.
148    */
149   public void clearRecordedRequests() {
150      if (recordedRequests != null)
151         recordedRequests.clear();
152   }
153
154   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
155   // Inner types
156   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
157
158   private record Route(String method, String path, RequestHandler handler) {
159      boolean matches(String m, String p) {
160         return (method == null || method.equalsIgnoreCase(m)) && (path == null || path.equals(p));
161      }
162   }
163
164   /**
165    * Fluent builder for {@link MockHttpTransport}.
166    *
167    * @since 9.2.1
168    */
169   public static final class Builder {
170
171      final List<Route> routes = new ArrayList<>();
172      RequestHandler fallback;
173      boolean recordRequests;
174
175      private Builder() {}
176
177      /**
178       * Registers a handler for the given HTTP method and path.
179       *
180       * @param method The HTTP method (e.g. {@code "GET"}). {@code null} matches any method.
181       * @param path The exact request path (e.g. {@code "/api/users"}). {@code null} matches any path.
182       * @param handler The handler. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
183       * @return This object.
184       */
185      public Builder on(String method, String path, RequestHandler handler) {
186         routes.add(new Route(method, path, handler));
187         return this;
188      }
189
190      /**
191       * Sets a fallback handler used when no route matches.
192       *
193       * <p>
194       * Without a fallback, unmatched requests receive a {@code 404} response.
195       *
196       * @param value The fallback handler. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
197       * @return This object.
198       */
199      public Builder fallback(RequestHandler value) {
200         fallback = value;
201         return this;
202      }
203
204      /**
205       * Enables recording of all received requests so they can be retrieved via
206       * {@link MockHttpTransport#getRecordedRequests()}.
207       *
208       * @return This object.
209       */
210      public Builder recordRequests() {
211         recordRequests = true;
212         return this;
213      }
214
215      /**
216       * Builds and returns the {@link MockHttpTransport}.
217       *
218       * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
219       */
220      public MockHttpTransport build() {
221         return new MockHttpTransport(this);
222      }
223   }
224}
